config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights must come first because they protect people from abuse by the majority or by the state. In my view, a society is healthier when it solves problems without treating personal freedom as disposable. Collective interests are important, but they should limit rights only in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan hiasan; itu perlindungan nyata agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak menginjak minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak dasar tetap kuat, lalu pembatasan hanya dipakai secara sempit, transparan, dan benar-benar perlu. Kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan kebebasan pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very wary of “for the common good” language, because it can easily be used to excuse pressure from the majority, officials, or local power holders against people who are already weak. In a diverse country like Indonesia, rights are what stop community or state interests from turning into injustice, especially for minorities. Society matters, but it is healthier when it protects individual rights first and only limits them in narrow, clearly necessary cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama kebaikan bersama. Dari pengalaman Amerika, saya melihat alasan “demi masyarakat” sering menjadi pintu masuk bagi pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk menekan minoritas, jadi hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang kuat. Masyarakat memang penting, tetapi masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak dasar tidak bisa dikorbankan seenaknya. Pembatasan hak hanya masuk akal kalau benar-benar sempit, jelas, dan perlu untuk mencegah bahaya nyata.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should generally outrank individual rights. In Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “the public good” or “social harmony” can be used to silence minorities, pressure dissenters, and let local power holders avoid accountability. Society is important, but it becomes dangerous when it treats individual rights as optional, because then the people with the least power pay the price. The better rule is that collective interests may justify narrow limits only when they are truly necessary, clearly defined, and not used as a cover for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari sudut pandang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan itu justru lebih mudah merugikan minoritas atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, pemerintah dan mayoritas akan terlalu leluasa menentukan siapa yang boleh dikorbankan. Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi hanya layak membatasi hak dalam keadaan yang sangat sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar diperlukan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
